pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the community foundation and routing users to key grant-related tasks.
primary_functions: Highlight main grant programs, upcoming deadlines, and featured opportunities.
Provide clear entry points to find grants, view the dashboard, manage saved grants, sign up for alerts, and contact support.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click primary "Find Grants" or "Open Grant Opportunities" call-to-action on the homepage hero.
Successful submission of the Sign Up form shown in a homepage modal or panel.
Click "Grant Email Alerts" or "Get Updates" link in the homepage content.
Click "Questions? Contact us" link in homepage support area.
Click "Learn more about the foundation" link in homepage overview.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Home" link in the global header navigation.
Default landing page when visiting the site root URL.

pages.name: Grants Search & Browse
pages.description: Unified page for browsing, filtering, and sorting both open and past grants.
primary_functions: Browse and filter open grant opportunities using focus area, award amount, deadline range, and other criteria, including an 'Open Grant Opportunities' section.
Switch to a 'Past Grants' section and filter by year awarded, focus area, and award amount range.
Sort results (e.g., by maximum award high-to-low or low-to-high, deadline soonest-first) and open individual grant detail pages.
Select grants to compare side-by-side and initiate a grant comparison view.

incoming_params.description: Determines which tab/section is active on load. Expected values: "open_grants" for Open Grant Opportunities or "past_grants" for Past Grants.
Optional comma-separated list of FocusArea.code values used to preselect focus area filters (e.g., "youth_families,education").
Optional minimum award amount used to prefill the award range filter for open grants.
Optional maximum award amount used to prefill the award range filter for open grants.
Optional starting deadline date (YYYY-MM-DD) for filtering open grants.
Optional ending deadline date (YYYY-MM-DD) for filtering open grants.
Optional sort key applied to the current section, e.g., "max_award_desc", "max_award_asc", "deadline_soonest".
Current page number for paginated results when sharing or revisiting a search.
Optional year filter applied when section=past_grants, corresponding to the "Year Awarded" dropdown.
Optional minimum amount filter for past grants (used with section=past_grants).
Optional maximum amount filter for past grants (used with section=past_grants).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a grant card or title in the Open Grant Opportunities results list.
Click "View Comparison" after selecting up to three past grants in the Past Grants section.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Grants" header link, which loads this page with section=open_grants.
Accessed via "Open Grants" (section=open_grants) and "Past Grants" (section=past_grants) links in the footer.
Search result URLs with encoded filters and section can be bookmarked or shared directly.

pages.name: Grant Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific grant program or opportunity.
primary_functions: Display complete grant information including description, eligibility, focus areas, award range, deadlines, and match required percentage.
Provide actions to start a new application for this grant.
Allow the user to save the grant to 'My Grants' or add it to an application plan (with an optional dialog to name the plan, such as 'Arts Priority 2025').

incoming_params.description: ID of the grant program whose full details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Start Application" button, after startGrantApplication successfully creates or resumes a draft.
Click "Ask an eligibility question" link on the grant detail page.
Click a "Back to open grants" link or breadcrumb.

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user navigates to a URL containing ?grantProgramId=ID, typically from search results or featured lists.
Linked from grant cards on the Grants Search & Browse page, dashboard, or homepage highlights.

pages.name: My Grants & Plans
pages.description: Central place to view and manage saved grants, application plans, and related items.
primary_functions: List grants that the user has saved or added to application plans, including items like the highest-paying youth grant or chosen arts grant.
Allow the user to organize saved grants into plans (e.g., view or rename 'Arts Priority 2025'), remove saved items, and start or resume applications from saved grants.

incoming_params.description: Optional. When provided, the page focuses on the specified application plan (e.g., 'Arts Priority 2025') on load.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the title of a saved grant to view its full details.
Click "Start Application" or "Continue Application" from a saved grant row or application list.
Click "Find more grants" from the My Grants empty state or sidebar.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "My Grants" link in the global header navigation.
Accessible via the "My Grants" link in the footer navigation.
Plan-specific views can be accessed directly via ?planId=PLAN_ID.

pages.name: Grant Comparison
pages.description: Side-by-side comparison view for selected grants, primarily for analyzing past grants.
primary_functions: Show key attributes of up to three selected grants (such as focus area, award amounts, year awarded, and other criteria) in a comparison table.
Allow the user to save the current comparison with a custom name (e.g., 'Health 2023 mid-range') for future reference.

incoming_params.description: Comma-separated list of up to three GrantAward.id values selected from the Past Grants results for comparison.
Optional ID of a previously saved GrantComparison to reload its configuration and notes.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to Past Grants" from the comparison view.

access_methods.description: Reached after clicking "View Comparison" on the Past Grants list when at least two grants are selected.
Saved comparison URLs containing grantAwardIds or comparisonId can be bookmarked and revisited.

pages.name: Account Dashboard
pages.description: Main account home view summarizing the user's activity and providing shortcuts to key account-related pages.
primary_functions: Provide an overview of saved grants, in-progress applications, and upcoming deadlines relevant to the user.
Offer navigation shortcuts to the Organization Profile, Account & Notification Settings, My Grants, Grants Search, and current applications.
Allow the user to initiate new applications or jump back into draft applications started from specific grants.

incoming_params.description: Optional dashboard section to focus on after navigation, e.g., "applications", "saved_grants", or "notifications".

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Find Grants" shortcut from the dashboard.
Click the "View all saved grants" link from the saved grants summary.
Click a draft or submitted application row in the applications list to view or edit.
Click the "Organization Profile" link shown for organization accounts.
Click the "Notification Settings" or "Account Settings" link from the dashboard.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Dashboard" link in the global header navigation.
Accessible via the "Dashboard" link in the footer navigation.
Users are redirected here after successfully creating an account via the Sign Up form.

pages.name: Organization Profile
pages.description: Page for creating and maintaining the user's organization profile for grant applications.
primary_functions: Capture and edit organization details such as Organization Name, Organization Type (e.g., '501(c)(3) Nonprofit'), and Annual Budget.
Allow the user to select and save Program Areas (e.g., 'Environment' and 'Neighborhoods') using a multi-select control.
Validate and save the organization profile so it can be used in future grant applications.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Save Profile" and then follow the redirect back to the dashboard.

access_methods.description: Accessed from a "Organization Profile" link in the dashboard or account menu.

pages.name: Application Form
pages.description: Multi-step grant application form for a specific grant, including applicant and project information.
primary_functions: Collect required fields for the application, including Applicant Information and Project details (e.g., Project Title, Requested Amount, Project Start Date).
Allow the user to save the application as a draft (e.g., 'Save Draft' or 'Save & Exit') and resume later from the dashboard or My Grants.
Provide a path to review and submit a completed application when all steps are finished.

incoming_params.description: ID of the GrantApplication to edit or submit. Created via startGrantApplication when first starting an application.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Save & Exit" or after a successful submission when choosing to return to the dashboard.
Click a "View program details" link from within the application form.

access_methods.description: Accessed with a required ?applicationId=ID parameter after starting or resuming an application.
Reached immediately after startGrantApplication is called from the Grant Detail or My Grants page.

pages.name: Account & Notification Settings
pages.description: Settings page for configuring notification preferences and related account options.
primary_functions: Enable or disable deadline reminder notifications and set the number of days before deadline (e.g., 14 days) for reminder emails.
Manage grant update notifications, keeping options for receiving email or in-site updates about grant status or changes in the on/selected state.
Control news and blog email notifications, including turning off email updates about news or blog posts and saving these preferences.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Save Settings" and follow the redirect back to the dashboard's notifications overview.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Account Settings" link in the footer navigation.
Accessible via account or profile menus from the dashboard.

pages.name: Grant Email Alerts Signup
pages.description: Standalone subscription page for grant-related email (and optional SMS) alerts.
primary_functions: Collect an email address and related preferences, such as geographic focus (e.g., 'Entire County'), areas of interest (e.g., 'Youth & Families' and 'Environment'), and email frequency (e.g., monthly).
Allow the user to choose and adjust notification methods (e.g., keep Email selected, deselect SMS/Text) and subscribe to alerts.

incoming_params.description: Optional comma-separated list of FocusArea.code values used to preselect interests (e.g., "youth_families,environment").

outgoing_connections.trigger: After successful subscription, click a "Browse current grants" link.
Click a "Return to Home" link after subscribing.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Email Alerts" link in the global header navigation.
Accessible via the "Email Alerts" link in the footer navigation.
Linked from homepage and About page calls-to-action such as "Grant Email Alerts" or "Get Updates".

pages.name: Contact & Help
pages.description: Support and contact page offering help resources and a contact form.
primary_functions: Provide general help information, support options, and foundation contact details.

Offer a contact form where the user can choose a topic (such as 'Eligibility Question'), select a grant program (e.g., 'Community Impact Grant'), enter their name, email, and a detailed message with project description, planned start date, and location.
Allow the user to choose a preferred response method (e.g., Email) and submit the inquiry.

incoming_params.description: Optional preselected topic value for the contact form, e.g., "eligibility_question".
Optional grant program to preselect in the "Grant program" dropdown, often passed from a specific Grant Detail page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Home" or "Return to homepage" link after submitting a message.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Contact" link in the global header navigation.
Accessible via the "Contact & Help" link in the footer navigation.
Specific contact topics or grant program questions can be deep-linked using topic and grantProgramId parameters.

pages.name: About the Foundation
pages.description: Informational page describing the mission and activities of the community foundation.
primary_functions: Explain the foundation's mission, geographic focus, and primary focus areas (such as Youth & Families, Environment, Neighborhoods, Health & Wellness, and Arts & Culture).
Provide context on how the grantmaking process works and direct users to relevant resources, such as finding grants or contacting the foundation for help.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Find current grant opportunities" link in the grantmaking process section.
Click a "Contact us" link for additional questions about the foundation or its programs.
Click a "Sign up for grant updates" or similar call-to-action.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "About" link in the footer navigation.
Linked from the homepage and possibly external communications to describe the foundation.

header_links.description: Homepage with overview and quick links to grants and account features
Browse and search open and past grants, starting with open opportunities
View and manage saved grants and application plans
Account dashboard with overview of applications, saved grants, and shortcuts
Subscribe to grant email alerts by focus area and frequency
Contact and help page with support form and information

footer_links.description: About the community foundation and its mission
Open grant opportunities with filters and search
Past grants awarded, with filters and comparison tools
Sign up for grant email alerts
Support and contact form for questions, including eligibility questions
Notification and account preference settings, including deadline reminders
